steps.title: Use the dispute clue
steps.content: The discrepancy is unsurprising because forest researchers regularly dispute one another's classifications. That means scientists do not broadly agree about the scope of the categories "mature" and "old growth."

steps.title: Name the lack of agreement
steps.content: Choice A means discussion, choice B means watchfulness, and choice C means curiosity or concern. "Consensus" means broad agreement, so "little consensus" matches researchers disputing the classifications. The answer is D.
